etymology.description: An ancient form depicting a brush placed upside down -> mistaken as 又, moved to top in later scripts -> final form 卒
etymology.interpretation: The original pictogram depicted a brush, symbolizing the end or completion of a task, as in finishing writing. This intuitive meaning lends itself to a sense of completion found in graduation or finality.

examples.chinese: 他刚刚从大学毕业(他剛剛從大學畢業)，对未来充满了期待(對未來充滿了期待)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gānggāng cóng dàxué bìyè, duì wèilái chōngmǎnle qīdài.
examples.english: He just graduated from university and is full of expectations for the future.

examples.chinese: 毕业典礼结束后(畢業典禮結束後)，我们进行了合影(我們進行了合影)。
examples.pinyin: Bìyè diǎnlǐ jiéshù hòu, wǒmen jìnxíngle héyǐng.
examples.english: After the graduation ceremony, we took group photos.

examples.chinese: 毕业生们穿着学士服(畢業生們穿著學士服)，站在台上接受祝福(站在臺上接受祝福)。
examples.pinyin: Bìyèshēngmen chuānzhe xuéshì fú, zhàn zài táishàng jiēshòu zhùfú.
examples.english: The graduates wore caps and gowns, standing on the stage to receive blessings.
